subject: Statistics
title: Naive Methods: The Baselines That Win
description: A quick win: understand Naive Methods: The Baselines That Win.

hook.text: The best forecast for tomorrow's stock price? Today's price. The naive method is shockingly hard to beat.

buildup.text: Naive forecasts use simple rules: repeat the last value, repeat last year's value, or use the historical average.

discovery.text: The seasonal naive method predicts this January using last January. For seasonal data, it's often very accurate.

twist.text: Models that can't beat the naive method are wasting effort. Always compare against these baselines.

climax.text: In the M3 forecasting competition, naive methods outperformed many sophisticated algorithms. Simplicity is underrated.

punchline.text: If you can't beat the naive forecast, your model isn't learning. Start simple.

quiz.question: Why should every forecasting model be compared to naive methods?
options: If a complex model can't beat the naive baseline, it's not adding value
Naive methods are always the most accurate
Complex models never work in practice
